name: 3D XYZ Chess
author: Rick Hewson (1988-2016) / AGP port
description: Rick Hewson's three-dimensional chess, developed and perfected over three decades (Abstract Games issue 24). A 4×4×4 cube of 64 spaces holds the standard 32 chess pieces: the armies fill 2×2×4 blocks at opposite vertical corners. Rook, Bishop and Queen make their regular chess moves extended through vertical as well as horizontal planes (no space-diagonals), the King steps one space orthogonally only, and the Knight steps one space triagonally — confined to a quarter of the board. Pawns can never change level: they step orthogonally on their level toward the opposite corner, capture exactly as they move (edge pawns may open with a two-space move), and promote to Queen at the far corner of their level. Win by checkmate; stalemate is a draw.
